attractions.name: 1857 Jackson House (Official Website)
attractions.description: 1857 Jackson House is an award-winning "elevated comfort food" restaurant and cocktail bar that recently relocated from downtown Dover to a new home in Middletown. Recognized as the "Best New Restaurant" for 2025 by Delaware Today, it combines historic charm with a creative, chef-driven menu and artisanal cocktails. (Official Website), (Delaware Today)
attractions.hours: Monday: 4:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Tuesday – Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Friday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant; menu prices apply) (Official Website)
highlights: 'Named "Best New Restaurant" for 2025 by Delaware Today ([Delaware Today)', 'Features the signature "Winner Winner Chicken Dinner," a roasted half-chicken feast with truffle mac & cheese (Instagram)', 'Offers a popular weekend brunch with unique dishes like Strawberry Cheesecake Pancakes and Açaí bowls (Official Website)', 'Diverse "Social Hour" at the bar and patio featuring craft cocktails and shareable plates (Official Website)', 'Menu highlights include premium entrees like Bison Steak, Passionfruit Salmon, and Lobster Bisque (Official Website)', 'Deep historical roots dating back to its original 1857 establishment in Dover (Bay to Bay News)']
attractions.tips: Monday is "Industry Night" with 20% off for hospitality workers, and the "Winner Winner Chicken Dinner" is 25% off all day on Mondays. Reservations are not required but are strongly encouraged for dinner and brunch. (Official Website), (Instagram)
attractions.nearby: The Everett Theatre, Middletown Historical Society, Augustine State Wildlife Management Area, Silver Lake Park (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: 9 East Taphouse
attractions.description: 9 East Taphouse is a premier bar and grill located in the heart of downtown Dover, offering a vibrant atmosphere and exceptional service. The venue features a diverse menu of burgers, steaks, and craft beers, while also serving as a popular spot for live entertainment and special events like drag brunches and line dancing (9 East Taphouse Official Site, Visit Central Delaware).
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 11:00 am – 1:00 am; Sunday: 9:00 am – 1:00 am; Closed Monday (Visit Central Delaware)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Visit Central Delaware)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Central Delaware)
highlights: 'Extensive menu featuring hand-rolled cheesesteak egg rolls, specialty mac & cheese, and fresh seafood ([Toast Tab)', 'Vibrant nightlife atmosphere with regular events such as line dancing and drag brunches (Visit Central Delaware, AllEvents)', 'Scenic views overlooking Mirror Lake and Silver Lake from its downtown location (Downtown Dover Partnership, Visit Central Delaware)', 'Spacious venue capable of accommodating weddings, group meetings, and other special events (Visit Central Delaware)', 'Weekend breakfast and brunch service available on Saturdays and Sundays (Visit Central Delaware)', 'Rotating selection of local craft beers and signature cocktails (9 East Taphouse Official Site)']
attractions.tips: Try the daily specials, which often feature fresh, locally sourced ingredients. For a more lively experience, visit during one of their scheduled entertainment nights like line dancing or the monthly drag brunch (Nova Circle, Visit Central Delaware). Note that as of late March 2026, the building has been reported as temporarily closed for maintenance (Visit Central Delaware).
attractions.nearby: The Old State House, Legislative Mall, Biggs Museum of American Art, and Johnson Victrola Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Air Mobility Command Museum
attractions.description: The Air Mobility Command Museum is the only museum in the United States dedicated to the history of military airlift and air refueling. Located in the historic Hangar 1301, the museum features a collection of over 35 restored aircraft and exhibits that tell the story of humanitarian and military aviation history. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Sunday, 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. Closed Mondays, Tuesdays, and all Federal Holidays except Veterans Day. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free admission and parking (Official Website)
highlights: 'Collection of over 35 restored aircraft including the C-5A Galaxy and C-47A Skytrain ([Official Website)', 'Historic Hangar 1301, listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Official Website)', 'Retired Dover AFB Control Tower cab offering views of the airfield (Wikipedia)', 'Commemoration Park, a tribute to military service members (Official Website)', 'Flight simulators providing an interactive aviation experience (Tripadvisor)']
attractions.tips: Access the museum only via the main entrance on Route 9, not through the Dover AFB gates. For the best experience, visit on the third Saturday of the month (April-October) for Open Cockpit Day to go inside many of the aircraft. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: John Dickinson Plantation, Johnson Victrola Museum, Old State House, Biggs Museum of American Art, First State Heritage Park (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Biggs Museum of American Art The BIGGS Museum
attractions.description: The Biggs Museum of American Art, established in 1993, houses one of the finest collections of American fine and decorative arts with a special focus on Delaware and the Mid-Atlantic region. Its permanent collection includes three floors of paintings, sculpture, silver, and furniture arranged chronologically to tell the story of American art from 1700 to the present. The BIGGS Museum, Incollect
attractions.address: 406 Federal Street, Dover, DE 19901 The BIGGS Museum
attractions.hours: Thursday – Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Closed Monday – Wednesday The BIGGS Museum
attractions.admission: Free Admission (Brought to you by Dover Federal Credit Union) The BIGGS Museum
highlights: 'Chronological galleries showcasing 300 years of American art history [Kupi.com', "One of North America's largest collections of works by illustrator Frank Schoonover Kupi.com", 'Extensive collection of Delaware silver, including the Colonel Kenneth P. and Regina I. Brown Collection Incollect', 'Masterpieces by the Peale family of artists and Federal Delaware furnishings Incollect', "Aloft (2015), a permanent sculptural installation by Erica Loustau on the museum's façade Clio", 'Community programs and events, including Thursday night programming The BIGGS Museum']
attractions.tips: Admission is free thanks to community sponsorship. Photography may be restricted in certain galleries, so check with staff. Free parking is available in the museum's parking lot and adjacent to the Legislative Mall. Plan for 1.5 to 2.5 hours to fully explore the collection. Tripadvisor, Kupi.com
attractions.nearby: Delaware Legislative Hall, The Old State House, Delaware Public Archives, First State Heritage Park, Johnson Victrola Museum, Silver Lake Park Downtown Dover Partnership, Kupi.com

attractions.name: Blue Ball Barn (Alapocas Run State Park)
attractions.description: The Blue Ball Barn is a historic two-story stone dairy barn built in 1914 by Alfred I. du Pont as part of the Nemours Estate. Now the centerpiece of Alapocas Run State Park, it serves as a multifunctional conference center and the permanent home of the Delaware Folk Art Collection. The barn is notable for its environmental design, being the first public building in Delaware to achieve LEED certification. Visit Delaware, Delaware State Parks
attractions.hours: Daily: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Museum/Office); Park Grounds: 8:00 AM – Sunset. Delaware State Parks
attractions.admission: Free for the museum; Park entrance fees (March 1 – November 30): $5 for Delaware-registered vehicles, $10 for out-of-state vehicles. Walking or biking into the park is free. Delaware State Parks
highlights: ['Home to the Delaware Folk Art Collection, featuring over 120 works by local artists.', 'First public building in Delaware to be LEED certified for environmental design.', "Historic 1914 stone architecture originally built for the du Pont family's Nemours Estate.", 'Features a large open banquet room in the former hayloft and a secluded outdoor courtyard.', 'Directly accessible from the Northern Delaware Greenway Trail.']
attractions.tips: Attend 'Folk Art Fridays' during summer months for art, music, and food trucks. Use the Willing Street parking lot for easier access to the park's hiking trails. Entrance fees only apply seasonally from March through November. Delaware State Parks, IN Wilmington
attractions.nearby: Northern Delaware Greenway Trail, Can-Do Playground, Nemours Estate, Rock Climbing Cliffs at Alapocas Run, Brandywine River, Brandywine Zoo. IN Wilmington

attractions.name: C&D Canal Museum
attractions.description: The C&D Canal Museum, housed in an original 18th-century pump house, depicts the history and operation of the canal connecting the Chesapeake and Delaware bays. It features original steam engines, a large waterwheel, and exhibits on the canal's construction and evolution into a major shipping passage. (USACE, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.; Weekends (Memorial Day - Labor Day): 11:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. (USACE)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Year-round); Weekends (Memorial Day to Labor Day, pending volunteers)
attractions.admission: Free (USACE)
highlights: ['Original 1850s steam engines and a massive waterwheel once used to lift water for the locks', 'Working model of a canal lock showing how vessels were raised and lowered', 'Collection of fossils and artifacts discovered during canal construction', "Detailed boat and bridge models representing the canal's maritime history", 'Bethel Bridge Lighthouse replica located on the museum grounds']
attractions.tips: The museum is self-guided and can typically be toured in about an hour. Be sure to check out the outdoor displays and the replica lighthouse nearby. (Yelp, USACE)
attractions.nearby: Chesapeake City Historic District, Bethel Bridge Lighthouse, Ben Cardin C&D Canal Trail, Chesapeake Inn Restaurant and Marina (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Cape Henlopen State Park
attractions.description: Cape Henlopen State Park is a 5,000-acre natural wonder located where the Delaware Bay meets the Atlantic Ocean. The park features a diverse coastal ecosystem with pristine beaches, mature maritime forests, and historical World War II landmarks. (Delaware State Parks)
attractions.hours: Park: 8:00 AM - Sunset daily; Park Office: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM (April-Oct: Daily; Nov-March: Mon-Fri) (Delaware State Parks)
attractions.admission: DE registered vehicle: $10/day, Non-DE registered vehicle: $20/day, Walking/biking: Free (Delaware State Parks)
highlights: 'Fort Miles Museum & Historical Area', 'Seaside Nature Center', '24-hour Fishing Pier', 'Walking Dunes Trail and extensive bike paths', 'Disc golf course and basketball courts', 'Guarded swimming beaches with bathhouses ([Delaware State Parks)']
attractions.tips: It is highly recommended to make camping reservations early due to high demand. Visitors can enjoy free entry when walking or biking into the park. Don't miss the panoramic views from the top of the World War II observation towers. (Thompson Island)
attractions.nearby: Lewes Beach, Cape May-Lewes Ferry, Gordon's Pond, Rehoboth Beach (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Capital City Farmers Market
attractions.description: The Capital City Farmers Market is a vibrant community hub in downtown Dover that connects local shoppers with Delaware farmers, food producers, and artisans. Visitors can find a variety of seasonally harvested produce, local meats, honey, and handmade crafts in a friendly, welcoming environment. The market also serves as a gathering point for the Dover community, offering authentic farm-to-table experiences and supporting the local agricultural economy (CropCart Markets).
attractions.hours: Wednesdays: 11:30 AM – 3:30 PM (Typical season June – October); Saturday mornings for special events and peak market buzz (CropCart Markets, Delaware News)
attractions.days_open: Wednesdays (Delaware News) or Saturdays (seasonal/special events) (Airial Travel)
attractions.admission: Free admission (Airial Travel)
highlights: 'Fresh, seasonally harvested produce from local Delaware farms ([CropCart Markets)', 'A wide selection of local meats, eggs, and artisanal goods (FarmersMarkets.io)', 'Homemade jams, honey, and specialty baked goods (FarmersMarkets.io)', 'Local artisans and crafters selling unique handmade jewelry and gifts (Airial Travel)', 'Participation in the "Buy Local Delaware" initiative to support the regional economy (CropCart Markets)', 'Acceptance of SNAP/EBT benefits for fresh food access (CropCart Markets)']
attractions.tips: Arrive early for the best selection of popular items like baked goods and specialty products which often sell out first. Bring reusable bags to support eco-conscious shopping, and note that while many vendors prefer cash, some accept credit cards and SNAP/EBT (CropCart Markets).
attractions.nearby: Old State House, First State Heritage Park, Biggs Museum of American Art, and Legislative Hall (Visit Delaware)

attractions.name: Christ Episcopal Church Official Site
attractions.description: Christ Episcopal Church is a historic parish in Dover, Delaware, founded in 1704 and housed in a brick structure dating back to 1734. It is a prominent site within the Dover Green Historic District and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. The church is renowned for its historic cemetery, which serves as the final resting place for many notable Delawareans and features a cenotaph for Caesar Rodney. Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 501 South State Street, Dover, DE 19901 Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Sunday Worship: 8:00 AM & 10:00 AM; Wednesday: 12:10 PM; Office Hours: Typically Monday - Friday (contact office for specific times) Official Site, Kent County Tourism
attractions.days_open: Sunday, Wednesday (Services); Monday - Friday (Office) Official Site, Weekly eNewsletter
attractions.admission: Free Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places [Wikipedia)', 'Features a cenotaph for Caesar Rodney, a signer of the Declaration of Independence Wikipedia)', 'Original brick structure completed in 1734 Official Site', 'Participant in the First State Heritage Park at Dover Official Site', "Active community outreach programs like the 'Blessing Box' and 'Neighbors at the Table' Official Site", 'Beautiful historic cemetery with self-guided tours available Official Site']
attractions.tips: Take advantage of the self-guided tour of the churchyard to learn about local history. Consider visiting during a First State Heritage Park lantern tour for a unique evening experience. The church is within an easy stroll of the historic Dover Green. Official Site, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Dover Green, Old State House, Legislative Hall, Biggs Museum of American Art, Delaware State Museum Tripadvisor

attractions.name: City of Rehoboth Beach
attractions.description: Rehoboth Beach is a quintessential Atlantic coast beach town known for its mile-long boardwalk, pristine beaches, and vibrant culinary scene. Often called "The Nation's Summer Capital," it offers a charming walkable downtown filled with boutiques, galleries, and award-winning restaurants. (City of Rehoboth Beach, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Beach/Boardwalk: Open 24 hours (rules apply at night); Museum (Summer): Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri 10am-4pm, Sat-Sun 10am-2pm, Closed Wed; Museum (Winter): Fri 10am-4pm, Sat-Sun 10am-2pm, Closed Mon-Thu (City of Rehoboth Beach, Rehoboth Beach Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Beach & Boardwalk access); Rehoboth Beach Museum: Free (donations appreciated) (City of Rehoboth Beach, Rehoboth Beach Museum)
attractions.phone: 302-227-6181 (City Hall), 302-227-7310 (Museum) (City of Rehoboth Beach, Rehoboth Beach Museum)
highlights: 'Mile-long wooden boardwalk lined with shops and arcades ([City of Rehoboth Beach)', 'Rehoboth Beach Bandstand featuring free summer concerts (Rehoboth Bandstand)', 'Rehoboth Beach Museum showcasing local history and artifacts (Rehoboth Beach Museum)', 'Award-winning dining scene from fine dining to "delightful dives" (City of Rehoboth Beach)', 'Scenic Junction Breakwater Trail for biking and hiking (City of Rehoboth Beach)', 'Funland amusement park located directly on the boardwalk (City of Rehoboth Beach)']
attractions.tips: Parking is free from September 16 to May 14; during the summer season, use the ParkMobile app for meter payments. Visit on Tuesdays (May-October) for the Farmers Market at Grove Park. (City of Rehoboth Beach)
attractions.nearby: Dewey Beach, Lewes, Cape Henlopen State Park, Tanger Outlets, Delaware Seashore State Park (City of Rehoboth Beach)

attractions.name: Cooch's Bridge Historic Site
attractions.description: Cooch's Bridge Historic Site is the location of Delaware's only Revolutionary War land battle, fought on September 3, 1777. The site encompasses the historic Cooch Homestead and the Cooch-Dayett Mill complex, representing centuries of agricultural and industrial history. It is currently transitioning from private ownership to a public historic site under the Delaware Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs. American Battlefield Trust, Official Site
attractions.address: 961 S. Old Baltimore Pike, Newark, DE 19702
attractions.hours: Closed to the public except for registered tours; group tours (8+) available by appointment. Official Site
attractions.admission: Free (registration required for tours) Official Site
highlights: "Site of the 1777 Battle of Cooch's Bridge, Delaware's only Revolutionary War land battle. [American Battlefield Trust", 'The Cooch Homestead, which remained in the Cooch family for seven generations. Official Site', 'The historic Cooch-Dayett Mill complex, illustrating early American industrialization. Official Site', 'Interpretive stories regarding the lives of both free and enslaved Black families on the property. Official Site', "Ongoing archaeological research and preservation efforts to uncover the site's military history. Friends of Cooch's Bridge"]
attractions.tips: Reservations are required for all visits as the site is not open for walk-ins. Tours last approximately one hour and are conducted entirely outdoors, so comfortable walking shoes are recommended. Note that there is currently no public access to the interiors of the historic house or mill. Official Site
attractions.nearby: Pencader Heritage Museum, Iron Hill Park, Hale-Byrnes House, Deer Park Tavern, White Clay Creek State Park. Tripadvisor, Main Street Newark

attractions.name: Delaware Art Museum
attractions.description: The Delaware Art Museum features a world-renowned collection of over 12,000 objects, focusing on American art from 1757 to the present and British Pre-Raphaelite art. It is also well-known for its extensive collection of American illustration, including works by Howard Pyle and the Brandywine School.
attractions.hours: Wednesday – Sunday: 10 am – 4 pm; Monday & Tuesday: Closed. Open Thursday evenings until 8 pm (April – December). Closed on New Year’s Day, Independence Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day.
attractions.admission: Adults: $18, Seniors (ages 65+): $16, Students (w/valid ID): $7, Youth (ages 7-18): $6, Children 6 and under: FREE, Museum Members: FREE. Admission is free from 4 pm – 8 pm on Thursdays (April through December).
highlights: ['British Pre-Raphaelites (The Samuel and Mary R. Bancroft Collection)', 'American Illustration (Howard Pyle and Storytelling)', 'American Art, 1757–1960 (Picturing America)', 'Copeland Sculpture Garden (Contemporary Art in a Natural Setting)', 'Contemporary Art (American Art since 1960)', 'Helen Farr Sloan Library & Archives']
attractions.tips: Visit on Thursday evenings (4 pm – 8 pm, April–December) for free admission. The museum has free on-site parking located behind the building and a nice snack bar and gift shop. It is compact enough to see in a couple of hours.
attractions.nearby: Rockford Park, Brandywine Park, Trolley Square, Nemours Estate, Winterthur Museum, Garden and Library.

attractions.name: Delaware Legislative Hall
attractions.description: Delaware Legislative Hall is the official state capitol building of Delaware, housing the chambers and offices of the Delaware General Assembly. Built between 1931 and 1933 in the Colonial Revival style, the red-brick structure serves as the heart of the state's legislative process. It is located on the historic Legislative Mall and contains numerous portraits, historical artifacts, and memorials dedicated to Delaware's history. (Delaware General Assembly, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Tuesday: 8:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Wednesday: 8:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Thursday: 8:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Friday: 8:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Delaware General Assembly)
attractions.admission: Free (Delaware General Assembly)
highlights: 'Chambers of the Delaware State Senate and House of Representatives ([Wikipedia)', 'Colonial Revival architecture designed by E. William Martin (Wikipedia)', 'Historical portraits and artifacts displayed throughout the building (Tripadvisor)', 'Memorials and monuments on the grounds, including the Delaware Women’s Suffrage Centennial monument (Delaware General Assembly)', 'Legislative Hall Café located on the ground floor (Delaware General Assembly)', 'Guided tours provided by First State Heritage Park (Delaware General Assembly)']
attractions.tips: Visitors must enter through the west door and present a government-issued photo ID to pass through security. Guided tours are typically available on weekdays between 10 a.m. and 1 p.m. through First State Heritage Park. Parking is conveniently located at the nearby Delaware Public Archives or the Tatnall Building. (Delaware General Assembly, Kent County Tourism)
attractions.nearby: Old State House, Biggs Museum of American Art, First State Heritage Park, Delaware Public Archives

attractions.name: Delaware Public Archives
attractions.description: The Delaware Public Archives serves as the official repository for the state's historical and government records, housing over 95,000 cubic feet of documents dating back to the 17th century. It offers public access to extensive genealogical resources, including vital statistics and military records, alongside rotating exhibits on Delaware's local history. Official Site
attractions.address: 121 Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd. North
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 9:00 am – 4:00 pm (Research Room), 8:00 am – 4:00 pm (Welcome Center); Closed Saturdays, Sundays, and State Holidays. Official Site
attractions.admission: Free admission for visitors and researchers; fees apply for specific duplication services and certified records. Source
highlights: ['Mabel Lloyd Ridgely Research Room for genealogy and history research', 'USS Delaware silver service set exhibit', 'Extensive collection of vital records (birth, marriage, death)', "Exhibits on Delaware's role in World War I and World War II", 'Public computers with free access to Ancestry.com and digital archives']
attractions.tips: Appointments for research are strongly encouraged to guarantee workspace and equipment. Visitors should bring a sweater as the research room is kept at a cool temperature. Free visitor parking is available on-site, and researchers can bring a USB flash drive to save scanned microfilm images for free. Source
attractions.nearby: Legislative Hall, Old State House, Biggs Museum of American Art, First State Heritage Park, Johnson Victrola Museum. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Delaware State Museum Buildings (Old Presbyterian Church Complex)
attractions.description: The Delaware State Museum Buildings, also known as the Old Presbyterian Church Complex, is a historic four-building site that has served as a center for state history and archaeology since 1950. The complex includes the 1790 Old Presbyterian Church, a 19th-century chapel, and the Eldridge Reeves Johnson Memorial Building, which houses the world-renowned Johnson Victrola Museum. Visitors can explore Delaware's prehistoric past through archaeological collections and discover the legacy of the Victor Talking Machine Company. (Wikipedia, Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs)
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Sunday: Closed (Note: Currently closed for major renovations; reopening Fall 2026) (Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs)
attractions.admission: Free (Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs)
highlights: '1790 Old Presbyterian Church, a prime example of early Federal architecture ([Wikipedia)', 'Johnson Victrola Museum, dedicated to the inventor of the Victrola phonograph (Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs)', 'Discover Archaeology exhibit featuring prehistoric artifacts from across Delaware (Delaware.gov)', 'Historic cemetery containing the graves of prominent figures like the Rev. John Miller (Delaware Public Archives)', 'Eldridge Reeves Johnson Memorial Building, designed in the Georgian style (Wikipedia)', '1880 Victorian-era chapel building originally used for Sunday school (SAH Archipedia)']
attractions.tips: Admission is free, though the complex is currently closed for significant preservation work and is expected to reopen in the Fall of 2026. Visitors can still explore the surrounding historic cemetery and are encouraged to check the official Delaware Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs website for tour updates. The museum is a key site within the "park without boundaries" known as the First State Heritage Park. (Division of Historical and Cultural Affairs, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: The Old State House, The Green, Delaware Public Archives, Legislative Hall, Biggs Museum of American Art (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Dover Green Historic District (NPS)
attractions.description: The Dover Green is a one-acre historic park established in 1717 that serves as the heart of Delaware's capital. It is famous as the site where Delaware ratified the U.S. Constitution in 1787 and served as a gathering point for revolutionary troops and civil rights demonstrations. Today, it is a key component of the First State National Historical Park and features preserved 18th and 19th-century architecture (NPS, Delaware Historical and Cultural Affairs).
attractions.hours: The Green (Park): Monday–Sunday, Sunrise–Sunset; Old State House: Wednesday–Sunday, 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM; John Bell House: Thursday–Saturday, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (NPS, First State Heritage Park).
attractions.days_open: The Green: Daily; Old State House: Wednesday–Sunday; John Bell House: Thursday–Saturday (NPS, First State Heritage Park).
attractions.admission: The Green and Old State House are free to visit (NPS); guided walking tours are $6.50 for adults, while children 8 and under are free (First State Heritage Park).
highlights: 'Site of the Golden Fleece Tavern where Delaware ratified the U.S. Constitution ([Delaware Historical and Cultural Affairs)', 'The Old State House, a Georgian-style building completed in 1792 (NPS)', 'The John Bell House, the oldest surviving timber-frame structure in Dover (First State Heritage Park)', 'Historical mustering ground for the Delaware Regiment during the American Revolution (Delaware Historical and Cultural Affairs)', 'Part of the multi-site First State National Historical Park (NPS)']
attractions.tips: Visitors can receive National Park Service passport stamps at the Old State House. Guided walking tours led by interpreters in period dress depart regularly from the John Bell House (NPS, First State Heritage Park).
attractions.nearby: Legislative Hall, Biggs Museum of American Art, John Dickinson Plantation, and the Johnson Victrola Museum (Delaware Historical and Cultural Affairs).

attractions.name: Dover Little Theatre
attractions.description: Dover Little Theatre is an intimate, 100-seat community theater established in 1933 by the Woman’s Club of Dover. The venue, a former undertaker’s establishment, is run entirely by volunteers and produces five mainstage productions each season, including plays and musicals. It serves as a home for local artists and theater enthusiasts, offering a close-knit theatrical experience in the Morris County area. Official Website, Visit NJ
attractions.hours: Friday & Saturday: 8:00 PM; Sunday Matinee: 2:00 PM Official Website
attractions.days_open: Friday, Saturday, and Sunday (during performance dates) Official Website
attractions.admission: Plays: $22, Musicals: $25 Official Website
highlights: ['Intimate 100-seat venue with excellent views of the stage from every seat.', 'Founded in 1933, making it one of the oldest continuously operating community theaters in New Jersey.', 'Entirely volunteer-run, utilizing the talents of local actors, directors, and designers.', 'Produces a diverse season featuring five mainstage productions, children’s theater, and special events.', 'Recipient of multiple NJACT Perry Award nominations for production quality.', 'A historic venue located in a quiet residential neighborhood in Dover.']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes early as there is no assigned seating; parking is available on the street or in a lot at the end of Elliott Street, which requires a slight uphill walk. Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: The Quiet Man (Irish Pub), Hedden Park, Rockaway Townsquare, Dover Area Historical Society Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Dover Public Library
attractions.description: The Dover Public Library serves as a community anchor and information center, providing inclusive programming and resources for lifelong learning. Its state-of-the-art 46,000 square foot facility, completed in 2012, is LEED Gold certified and houses diverse collections for all ages. (Official Website), (EDiS Company)
attractions.hours: Mon: 10:00am - 5:00pm; Tues - Thurs: 9:00am - 8:00pm; Fri & Sat: 10:00am - 5:00pm; Sun: 1:00pm - 5:00pm (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: '46,000 square foot state-of-the-art facility completed in 2012 ([EDiS Company)', 'LEED Gold Certified building featuring a sustainable green roof and geothermal HVAC system (Becker Morgan Group)', "Extensive children's department with a storytelling area and a dedicated teen loft section (Becker Morgan Group)", 'Large multi-purpose rooms and community meeting spaces (Official Website)', 'Designated Small Business Center and Job Center walk-in resources (Official Website)', 'Outdoor reading garden and performance area (Becker Morgan Group)']
attractions.tips: Free two-hour on-street parking is available nearby, and patrons can also utilize the Loockerman Way lot using the ParkMobile app. The library is located downtown between City Hall and the Post Office. (Downtown Dover Partnership), (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Dover City Hall, Old Post Office, Legislative Hall, Biggs Museum of American Art, and Memorial Park (Downtown Dover Partnership)

attractions.name: First State Heritage Park
attractions.description: First State Heritage Park is Delaware’s first urban "park without boundaries," linking nearly two dozen historic and cultural sites in the heart of Dover. The park showcases the state's rich history as the first to ratify the Constitution through costumed living history, thematic walking tours, and special events. Centered around the historic Dover Green, it offers a collaborative experience between state and city agencies to bring the capital's heritage to life. Delaware State Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 43 The Green (John Bell House) / 121 Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd. North (Welcome Center)
attractions.hours: Park Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Welcome Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM; John Bell House: Thu-Sat 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Legislative Hall: Wed-Fri 10:00 AM - 1:00 PM; First Saturdays: 9:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Most sites closed on Sundays. Delaware State Parks, Visit Central Delaware, State of Delaware
attractions.admission: Admission to all park sites is Free; Guided Walking Tours are $6.50 per person for participants older than 8. Visit Central Delaware, Tripadvisor
highlights: ['John Bell House Interpretive Center', 'Legislative Hall (State Capital building)', 'The Old State House', 'Johnson Victrola Museum', 'First Saturdays in the First State monthly events', 'Delaware Public Archives']
attractions.tips: Begin your visit at the Welcome Center & Galleries for maps and information. Free all-day parking is available at the Welcome Center at 121 Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd. North. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the 'park without boundaries' layout requires walking between various historic buildings and natural areas. Tripadvisor, State of Delaware
attractions.nearby: Biggs Museum of American Art, Woodburn (The Governor's Mansion), Hall House, John Dickinson Plantation, and the St. Jones River. Delaware State Parks, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Grey Fox Grille and Public House
attractions.description: Grey Fox Grille and Public House is a charming restaurant and bar located in a beautifully restored 1881 Victorian home in historic downtown Dover. It offers a unique dining experience with a menu of American classics, craft cocktails, and a selection of 16 beers on tap. The atmosphere is upscale yet casual, featuring multiple dining rooms and a pet-friendly outdoor patio. Grey Fox Grille, Visit Delaware

attractions.name: Woodburn, The Governor's Residence
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic House Museum, Official Residence
attractions.description: Woodburn is the official residence of the Governor of Delaware and is regarded as one of the state's finest examples of Middle Period Georgian architecture. Built around 1798 by Charles Hillyard III, the mansion has hosted notable figures including U.S. Senators and abolitionists before being purchased by the state in 1965. It serves as both a private residence and a historic landmark within the First State Heritage Park. (Official Website, SAH Archipedia)
attractions.hours: Public tours are currently not available on a regular basis; tours occur during scheduled special events and programs hosted by First State Heritage Park. (Official Website, First State Heritage Park)
attractions.days_open: Varies (Accessible only during special events or programs) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website, Visit Delaware)
highlights: 'Built c. 1798, it is a premier example of Middle Period Georgian architecture in Delaware. ([SAH Archipedia)', 'Has served as the official residence of the Governor of Delaware since its acquisition in 1965. (Official Website)', 'Features a magnificent 41-foot-long Federal stairhall known as the Great Hall. (Official Website)', 'Historically served as a stop on the Underground Railroad under the ownership of Quaker abolitionist Daniel Cowgill. (SAH Archipedia)', 'The grounds feature a tradition of tree planting by governors, including a Tulip poplar descendant from George Washington’s Mount Vernon. (Official Website)', "Houses the 'Wall of First Ladies,' featuring portraits of the governors' spouses who have lived in the mansion. (Official Website)"]
attractions.tips: As Woodburn is a working executive residence, public access is strictly limited to scheduled events; always check the First State Heritage Park calendar or the official website for tour availability. Security is present on-site, and photography of the interior may be restricted during certain events. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)

Established in 1937, it serves as a critical breeding ground and stopover for migratory birds along the Atlantic Flyway. The refuge features a diverse mix of freshwater impoundments, upland habitats, and salt marshes that support a wide variety of wildlife. U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

Visitors can explore the historic mansion, reconstructed outbuildings, and learn about the plantation's complex history through guided tours and exhibits State of Delaware, Wikipedia.

attractions.hours: Wednesday-Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Holiday Mondays: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Closed: Mondays, Tuesdays, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and Easter Sunday Delaware Children's Museum
attractions.admission: General Admission: $15.00; Members and children under 12 months: Free; CAPs Nights (3rd Wednesday of each month): $2.00 from 5pm-8pm Delaware Children's Museum
highlights: ["Stratosphere: A massive 30-foot tall climbing structure located in the museum's atrium.", 'Training Camp: An exhibit focused on the science of sports where kids can test their skills.', 'The Power of Me: An interactive exhibit exploring the human body and health.', 'Eco-Station: A place to learn about the environment and native Delaware species.', 'Bank on It: A financial literacy exhibit where kids can learn about money and math.', 'Studio D: An art studio for creative projects and self-expression.']
attractions.tips: Tickets must be purchased in person as advanced online tickets are not currently available. Free parking is available in Lot K at Beech Street and Shipyard Drive (use 601 S Madison Street for GPS) Delaware Children's Museum.
attractions.nearby: Riverwalk Mini Golf, Constitution Yards Beer Garden, Chase Center on the Riverfront, and the Wilmington Riverwalk Greater Wilmington CVB
